title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions & Economic Data Shape Markets
subtitle: April 9, 2026

sections.title: Market Pulse: Futures Edge Lower Amid Ceasefire Uncertainty

items.headline: US Futures Decline as Doubts Emerge Over Iran Ceasefire
items.summary: US stock futures edged lower on Thursday, April 9, 2026, as uncertainty surrounding the two-week ceasefire agreement between Iran and the US-Israeli side weighed on investor sentiment. This comes after a significant relief rally on Wednesday following the initial ceasefire announcement. Oil prices, which plunged yesterday, showed a modest recovery today, adding to the cautious mood. Asian markets also traded lower amid these doubts.

sections.title: Major Indices: Wednesday's Surge Followed by Thursday's Modest Dip
Top Movers: Airlines Soar, Energy Plummets (Apr 8)

items.headline: Delta Air Lines
items.summary: Airline stocks surged due to eased concerns over the Strait of Hormuz and declining oil prices following the US-Iran ceasefire.

items.headline: Carnival Corp.
items.summary: Cruise operator jumped as the ceasefire trimmed losses sustained since the Iran war began on fuel cost concerns.

items.headline: Levi Strauss & Co.
items.summary: Shares rallied after reporting a strong Q1 earnings beat with consolidated revenue up 14% year-over-year.

items.headline: Exxon Mobil Corp.
items.summary: Energy shares saw a massive deleveraging as crude oil prices plummeted 16% on Wednesday following the ceasefire, unlocking the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Occidental Petroleum Corp.
items.summary: Fell sharply as the war premium on oil dissipated with the ceasefire agreement and a significant drop in crude prices.

items.headline: Tamboran Resources Ltd.
items.summary: Shares plunged after the company priced a public offering to raise approximately $103.5 million for drilling and other corporate purposes.

sections.title: Market News: Fed Minutes, GDP Revision, and Global Growth

items.headline: Fed Minutes Show Growing Openness to Rate Hikes Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Minutes from the U.S. Federal Reserve's March meeting, released Wednesday, April 8, indicated a growing willingness among policymakers to consider interest rate hikes. This shift is attributed to a revised 2026 inflation outlook, exacerbated by war-related oil shocks. More officials mentioned the possibility of rate hikes, though the ceasefire complicates the Fed's decision-making. The Fed kept its key rate unchanged at approximately 3.6% in March.

items.headline: Q4 2025 GDP Third Estimate Due Today; Previous Revision Showed Slowdown
items.summary: The third estimate for the fourth quarter of 2025 GDP is expected today, April 9, 2026. The second estimate had revised down annualized GDP growth to 0.7%, reflecting downward adjustments to exports, consumer spending, government spending, and investment. February payrolls also saw a decline of 92,000, and January's core PCE inflation was confirmed at 3.1% year-over-year.

items.headline: Sectors See Rotation: Industrials Lead Rally, Energy Stocks Face Headwinds
items.summary: On Wednesday, industrials, communication services, and materials led gains, with ten of the eleven S&P sectors closing higher. Technology stocks, including Meta Platforms (+6.5%), Amazon (+3%), Alphabet (+3%), Nvidia (+2%), and Apple (+2%), were also strong performers. Conversely, energy stocks were the sole percentage losers, dropping 3.7%, as oil prices plummeted due to the ceasefire. In March, the energy sector was the only one in the S&P 500 to show gains, up 11.9%.

items.headline: World Bank Projects India's FY27 Growth at 6.6%, Cites Middle East Conflict Risks
items.summary: The World Bank's latest economic update, released today, April 9, 2026, projects India's growth at 6.6% in fiscal year 2027. This represents a slowdown, primarily due to higher energy prices caused by the Middle East conflict and associated supply chain disruptions. Despite the deceleration, India is expected to remain among the fastest-growing major economies globally.

items.headline: PriceSmart Reports Strong Q2 2026 Earnings
items.summary: PriceSmart announced its Q2 2026 earnings on April 8, 2026, reporting an 8.2% year-over-year increase in revenue. The growth was driven by new club openings and same-store sales, with a focus on private-label brands and ancillary services contributing to improved margins.

items.headline: 111 Inc. Reports Q4 Loss Amid Revenue Growth
items.summary: Chinese digital healthcare platform operator, 111 Inc. ($YI), reported a Q4 loss of $2.3 million, or 20 cents per share, on Thursday, April 9, 2026. The company posted revenue of $403.3 million for the quarter.
